what_it_does: This file is a terrain ledger entry that describes the obsidian-overlord repository, which actually packages substrate-mcp: a local-only MCP server that indexes markdown vaults into an address-graph, an autopoietic type-graph, and a Chroma semantic face, and exposes read/search/write/config tools to AI agents.
observations: The folder is named obsidian-overlord but the package is substrate-mcp
It exposes 48 tools (README prose still says 24)
It is live inside Company Claude sessions as the substrate-mcp tool surface
It uses the same local perplexity-ai/pplx-embed-context-v1-4b embedding endpoint as the Company
Ten vaults are registered, with 9,790 addresses and 116,726 chunks
There are zero code references to /home/tim/company in either direction
Git history is a single initial commit from 2026-05-19; all later runtime state is uncommitted drift
The .state/substrate.db is 5.4G with a 70MB WAL and was modified today
Coverage is thin: only 4 of 11,831 files were read line-by-line
standouts: Operational/runtime connection rather than code import or config reference
Package name (substrate-mcp) does not match folder name (obsidian-overlord)
Massive uncommitted runtime and content drift on a repo with only one git commit
Shared MCP substrate present in the same Claude sessions as the Company
Points at the same local pplx-embed-context-4b embedding endpoint
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry YAML frontmatter schema with type, register, relation, kind, status, coverage
Uses honest-status style flagging of uncommitted drift
Records git remote, path, size, and last_active evidence
References related ledger nodes via wikilink-style relates-to
concerns: Substantial uncommitted runtime and content drift is not reflected in version control
Git last_active is misleading because activity is all uncommitted
README tool count (24) contradicts generated tool count (48)
Thin source coverage means most of the 14 modules are only listed, not read
notes: A briefing note: this entry maps the obsidian-overlord folder (substrate-mcp) as a live, runtime-connected MCP memory substrate indexing ten vaults, and it explicitly flags a large uncommitted-drift gap.
questions: What does relation=connected precisely entail compared to an import or reference edge?
Which specific Company Claude sessions host this MCP server?
Do any of the ten indexed vaults contain Company data, even though /home/tim/company is not indexed?
How does the autopoietic type-graph actually mutate?
Why is there only one commit despite months of active runtime and CHANGELOG updates?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a descriptive terrain entry for a connected engine; actual content is a detailed operational audit of substrate-mcp including config, stores, vaults, and drift. It matches.
apparent_themes: Runtime wiring over static code linkage
Local-first AI memory and knowledge substrate
Operational/session co-presence as a connection form
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to document that substrate-mcp is a live shared memory substrate available to Company sessions, distinct from a code dependency, and to warn that its git history does not reflect its current runtime state.
novelty: It treats an MCP server as a terrain engine connected by session/runtime co-presence rather than by import, reference, or contains relationship, and it explicitly records a package/folder name mismatch plus uncommitted drift.
contribution: Provides a precise operational map of a live MCP substrate (true package name, entry points, store schema, registered vaults, embedding config, scale, and activity evidence) and an honest uncommitted-drift flag that a fused inventory would need to keep accurate.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain ledger entry for the obsidian-overlord repository, which packages substrate-mcp, a local MCP server that indexes markdown vaults into SQLite and Chroma stores and exposes search, read, write, and config tools inside Company Claude sessions.
intention_for_embedding: Record the runtime connection, configuration, scale, and uncommitted drift of a shared local MCP memory substrate.
